stem: A right rectangular prism has a length of 18 centimeters (cm), a width of 24 cm, and a height of 30 cm. What is the surface area, in cm², of the right rectangular prism?
rationale: The correct answer is 3384. The surface area of a right rectangular prism is 2(LW + LH + WH) = 2(18×24 + 18×30 + 24×30) = 2(432 + 540 + 720) = 2(1,692) = 3,384 cm².
